title: Erdős #349 culminating assembly: integer_isGoodPair_iff
informal_statement: The culminating assembly of Erdos #349's integer-characterization cluster: for positive integers t, alpha, the pair (t, alpha) is good (eventually every integer is a finite subset-sum of floor(t*alpha^n)) if and only if t=1 and alpha=2. This gives a full characterization of good pairs among POSITIVE INTEGERS specifically -- it does NOT resolve Erdos #349's general open question over all reals t, alpha (which remains genuinely OPEN).
informal_proof_idea: Combines four sublemmas via case split: (=>) if the pair is good, then alpha != 1 (else alpha_le_one_not_isGoodPair contradicts), alpha <= 2 (else alpha_gt_two_not_isGoodPair contradicts), so alpha = 2; then t <= 1 (else int_coeff_ge_two_not_isGoodPair contradicts), so t = 1. (<=) if t=1, alpha=2, apply one_two_isGoodPair directly.
formal_statement_pp: theorem erdos_349_integer_is_good_pair_iff : ∀ (t α : ℤ), 1 ≤ t → 1 ≤ α → ((∀ᶠ k in Filter.atTop, k ∈ {n : ℤ | ∃ B : Finset ℤ, ↑B ⊆ Set.range (fun n : ℕ ↦ ⌊(t:ℝ) * (α:ℝ) ^ n⌋) ∧ n = ∑ i ∈ B, i}) ↔ t = 1 ∧ α = 2)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this packet proves a full characterization of good pairs among POSITIVE INTEGERS for Erdos #349's integer_isGoodPair_iff, not a resolution of #349's general open question over all reals t, alpha (which remains genuinely OPEN per the sibling repo's own whitepaper). Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 5edbb136, problem_version f2c5cd7e-377a-42c5-a871-0785d3a4c2aa), a single Solve step (proof_format raw_lean_block), kernel_verified on the FIRST attempt despite being by far the largest single proof transported in this corpus's frontier lane (280 LOC, ~210 tactics) -- it combines all four previously-packetized assembly-piece sublemmas (int_coeff_ge_two_not_isGoodPair, alpha_le_one_not_isGoodPair, one_two_isGoodPair, alpha_gt_two_not_isGoodPair) re-embedded as local `have`s within one theorem, exactly mirroring the sibling repo's own structure, since the tracked pipeline verifies each problem_version in isolation with no cross-problem-version import mechanism. Each embedded sublemma's tactic script was independently debugged and kernel-verified as its own standalone packet earlier this session (frontier.erdos.erdos_349_int_coeff_ge_two_not_is_good_pair.v1, frontier.erdos.erdos_349_alpha_le_one_not_is_good_pair.v1, frontier.erdos.erdos_349_one_two_is_good_pair.v1, frontier.erdos.erdos_349_alpha_gt_two_not_is_good_pair.v1), which substantially de-risked this combined attempt. root_statement_hash did not match the hash cited in the sibling repo's own source comment (same as the int_coeff_ge_two packet -- see that packet's notes), so statement_fidelity is recorded as adapted_with_review. Independently re-verified in THIS environment's own pinned toolchain rather than treated as evidence merely because the sibling repo (F:\Github\mnehmos.llm-driven-proof-search.environment\ErdosProblems\erdos-349\proof\Erdos349_integer_isGoodPair_iff.lean) already reports it kernel_verified there -- per this repo's evidence policy. training.eligibility: quarantined per the default-quarantine policy for open_problem_related packets pending real external review. Ninth packet in the frontier/erdos lane; this completes the #349 integer_isGoodPair_iff cluster's headline theorem (6/7 of the original 7-file breakdown now packetized, counting this culminating assembly plus the four sublemmas plus the exists_finset_sum_two_pow sublemma -- only the sibling repo's own auxiliary infrastructure files, if any remain unlisted, would be left).
